hints: Think “what the app remembers”: saved shortcuts, tucked-away scraps, a snacky little token sites can leave behind, and a trail of pages you might clear in settings.
Same ring-based contest: hand protection, the roped stage, timed chunks of the bout, and the sound that often ends a chunk.
Read them as verbs (or verb-like uses): each can describe leaning, tipping, or slanting off-center—not plumb, not upright.
Prefix game: the same short lead-in, spoken aloud, turns all four into familiar compound words—try sliding it in front of each.
